videos.title: EXPLORING THE MODERN AREAS OF MUMBAI 🇮🇳 I AM SURPRISED! (Marine Drive, BKC, Malabar Hill)
videos.description:
Mumbai is richest city in India and the 12th richest city in the world. It’s full of millionaires and billionaires.

In my last video we explored the poorest area of Mumbai, Dharavi, which is also the biggest slum in India

You can watch that video here: 

➡ Inside India's Biggest Slum 🇮🇳 (Extreme conditions) https://youtu.be/L8H_I-JvJgQ 

But in today’s video we will explore the RICH and MODERN side of Mumbai.

We will visit Malabar Hill, Altamount road (Billionaire Row), lower Parel, BKC and more..

We will also visit some of the most popular and touristic areas of Mumbai such as Marine Drive, Gateway of India and The Taj Palace.

Let me know down below your thoughts about Mumbai.

Thanks for watching 🙏🏻🇮🇳

Best,
Gabi 

Khan Taxi driver WhatsApp: +91 86919 24224


My previous videos:

➡ Inside India's Biggest Slum 🇮🇳 (Extreme conditions) https://youtu.be/L8H_I-JvJgQ

videos.title: Places To Visit In Mumbai | Mumbai Darshan | Mumbai Tourist Places | Mumbai Trip #mumbai
videos.description:
Places To Visit In Mumbai | Mumbai Darshan | Mumbai Tourist Places | Mumbai Trip #mumbai 

Join our upcoming trips : 9034905765

Mumbai is undoubtedly the commercial capital of India and is one of the predominant port cities in the country. Mumbai's nature as the most eclectic and cosmopolitan Indian city is symbolized in the presence of Bollywood within the city, the centre of the globally-influential Hindi film and TV industries. It is also home to India's largest slum population. You can find cheap as well as luxury hotels and hostels

videos.title: Mumbai Tourist Places | Mumbai Darshan | Mumbai Complete Travel Guide | Mumbai Tour Plan
videos.description:
If you are Planning to Visit Mumbai, we've provided all the info related to Mumbai Trip | Mumbai Budget Tour | Mumbai Tourist Places | Top places to visit in Mumbai and Mumbai Darshan.

Mumbai is the financial capital of India and is the capital of Maharashtra state. It is also known as the city of dreams. You can plan a 3-4 days trip to this beautiful city and explore its wonderful hospitality, culture, food and tourist attractions.

In this video, we have covered Complete Mumbai Tour Guide and Plan with 3 days itinerary. We have provided every information related to a tourist spot like Entry Fees, Timings, Location etc.
मुंबई में घूमने की बहुत सी जगह है। हमने इस वीडियो में सभी को अच्छे से दिखाया है।

If you wish to do Mumbai Darshan, there are many places to visit in Mumbai which you must not miss. Top attractions in Mumbai include Gateway of India, Taj Mahal Hotel, Elephanta Caves, Colaba Causeway, Jehangir Art Gallery, Prince of Wales Museum, Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus (CSMT Railway Station), Oval Maidan, Rajabai Clock Tower, National Centre for Performing Arts (NCPA), Marine Drive, Hanging Gardens, Mumba Devi Temple, Haji Ali Dargah, Mahalakshmi Temple, Shree Siddhivinayak Temple, Bandra Fort, Bandra Worli Sea Link, Mount Marry Church, Bandstand Promenade, Iskcon Temple Juhu, Dadasaheb Phalke Chitranagari (Film City), Sanjay Gandhi National Park Mumbai, Kanheri Caves, Global Vipassana Pagoda, Worli Fort and Mumbai Zoo.

▶ Google Maps Link for all Tourist Attractions in Mumbai 👉 https://www.google.com/maps/d/u/0/edit?mid=1v_oa-5_Mo6oYhVJN4hiLXRfHcVK5c00&usp=sharing

Famous Beaches in Mumbai are Juhu Chowpatty Beach Mumbai, Girgaon Chowpatty Mumbai, Aksa Beach, Madh Island Beach, Gorai Beach and Versova Beach.

▶ Links

videos.title: 15 Things to do in MUMBAI 🇮🇳 | Travel Guide
videos.description:
Discover The Savvy Traveler Guides: https://bit.ly/4iB0oAl
Our recommendation for Tours & Activities: https://bit.ly/4f3ygEf
---
Mumbai isn't just a city; it’s a vibrant tapestry woven with endless stories and spirited energy. Navigating through its streets is like turning the pages of a fascinating novel, one where skyscrapers and slums, luxury and simplicity, history and modernity coexist in a curious harmony. This bustling metropolis, the heartbeat of India, is a melting pot of cultures, cuisines, and contrasts. As the city that never sleeps, Mumbai has a way of embracing you with one arm and surprising you with the other. In this video we’ll take you through the 15 things to do in Mumbai.

#mumbai #india #indiatravel 
---

videos.transcript: [Music] Mumbai isn't just a city it's a vibrant tapestry woven with endless stories and spirited energy navigating through its streets is like turning the pages of a fascinating novel one where skyscrapers and slums luxury and simplicity history and modernity coexist in a curious Harmony this bustling Metropolis the heartbeat of India is a Melting Pot of cultures Cuisines and contrasts as the city that never sleeps Mumbai has a way of embracing you with one arm and surprising you with the the [Music] other Standing Tall against the backdrop of the Arabian Sea the Gateway of India is an iconic landmark and a time portal this colossal Archway holds stories of colonial past and modern hustle it's like the city's very own welcoming committee Grand and dignified from here you can catch cool sea breezes and watch boats bobbing on the water while soaking up mumbai's determined spirit [Music] the Taj Mahal Palace isn't just a hotel it's an emblem of mumbai's luxury and resilience with its blend of moish Oriental and Florentine Styles the hotel is more than just a place to stay it's a piece of History the service here topnotch it's like stepping into a world where every detail is curated for elegance and comfort plus the view of the Gateway of India is just a cherry on top Marine Drive is the city's sparkling necklace especially when the lights come on at night this sea shaped Boulevard is where the sea meets the urban hustle a walk here with the breeze in your hair and the city's rhythm in your ears is like a dialogue with Mumbai itself it's a place to feel the pulse of the city whether you're watching the sunset or simply soaking in the [Music] views the bandra worldly seed link isn't just a Marvel of engineering it's a symbol of modern Mumbai driving across this cable State Bridge offers panoramic views of the Arabian Sea almost like you're gliding on [Music] [Applause] water tucked away in the sanj Gandhi National Park the caner caves are a testament to ancient craftsmanship this network of Buddhist caves chiseled out of solid Basalt tells Tales of a bygone year wandering through these caves is like leafing through pages of History written in stone it's a serene escape from the city's frenzy a place where history Whispers from the [Music] walls a short fairy ride from the mainland elephanta island is like a hidden chapter from mumbai's past the ancient caves here are a spectacle of Rock Cut art and architecture each sculpture and carving more mesmerizing than the last the Tranquility of the Island offer offers a stark contrast to the city's bustle making it a must visit for anyone intrigued by history and art wander through the cavernous spaces touch the cool Stone and feel the Echoes of ancient Artisans at [Music] work this Museum housed in an impressive Indo sarnik building is a treasure Trove of art history and archaeology each exhibit here narrates a different story it's like a walk through time where each room unveils a new facet of India's Rich Heritage stroll through galleries showcasing Exquisite paintings intricate sculptures and a fascinating array of Armory and textiles from the Grandeur of the mobile Emperors to the delicate craftsmanship of ancient artists it's a captivating journey through India's vibrant and diverse cultural [Music] landscape the Dr Bal daaji lad Museum with its Renaissance Revival architecture is a visual Delight inside the city's past comes alive through its stunning collection of maps photographs and models it's like a love letter to mumbai's Evolution showcasing the city's transformation from a cluster of seven islands to the bustling Metropolis it is [Music] today the isan temple in Mumbai is more than just a spiritual Haven it's a peaceful Retreat amidst the City's chaos with its beautiful idols and Serene Ambience the temple offers a chance to pause and reflect it's a spot where spirituality and Tranquility meet offering a slice of Solace for anyone seeking a moment of [Music] Peace this golden d pagod a symbol of peace and Harmony stands out in mumbai's landscape the meditation sessions here are like a bomb for the weary Soul offering a serene escape from the city's Relentless pace [Music] the VES and tourists alike flock here drawn by the spiritual energy and the Striking architecture inside the air buses with devotion and the DOR of the red gcha Idol is considered [Music] auspicious Manny bavin once the Mumbai headquarters of Mahatma Gandhi is a journey through India's struggle for Freedom each room are fact and photograph here tell a story of courage and nonviolence it's like walking through the pages of History getting an intimate glimpse into the life of a man who shaped the nation's Destiny explore Gandhi's living quarters the library full of his writings and Exhibits that chronical pivotal moments in India's fight for [Music] independence though beat is a spectac of color and activity often hailed as the world's largest outdoor laundry watching the dois or washerman at work is like witnessing a choreographed performance splashing water beating clothes and air drying them in neat rows it's a unique glimpse into the city's working heart a testament to the industrial spirit that keeps Mumbai ticking this place isn't just a laundry spot it's a living breathing part of mumbai's daily Rhythm the synchronization skill and sheer human effort that goes into washing thousands of garments daily is nothing short of remarkable [Music] P the topa hill the mount Mary Basilica is a beautiful Roman Catholic Church famous for the weekl long vandra Fair which takes place here every year in September stained glass windows cast a kaleidoscope of light across the benches while the ornate altar speaks of centuries of devotion the air here is thick with whispered prayers and the gentle scent of [Music] incense [Music] the bandra fair is a medley of colors flavors and sounds held annually at the mount Mary Basilica it's a fusion of religious celebration and a vibrant street fair from mouthwatering street food to eclectic stall selling everything under the sun the fair is a microcosm of mumbai's diverse and Lively culture diving into mumbai's culinary scene is like embarking on an endless adventure of flavors from the sizzling food stalls to the high-end restaurants offering Gourmet interpretations of local dishes mumbai's food landscape is a paradise for Foodies it's a city where every meal is an exploration of India's Traditions influences and Innovation the spicy Street chots the comforting warmth of a b path and the sumptuousness of a seafood thol by the coast each flavor captures the essence of Mumbai Mumbai is not just a city you visit it's an experience you live every corner has its own Rhythm and every street its own story it's a place where dreams are chased where the past and present coexist and where the spirit of India comes alive in its most dynamic form Mumbai doesn't just touch your heart it grabs it with both hands inviting you back even before you've left the city's magic lies in its ability to be many things at once chaotic yet Charming overwhelming yet inviting it's a city that never fails to surprise always revealing new layers and hidden alleys Beck and you to return and explore its endless [Music] depths

How to plan a trip to Mumbai
Best places to visit in Mumbai
Best things to do in Mumbai
How to save money in Mumbai
videos.transcript: with a rich history and culture Mumbai is a fascinating City to visit this is Katie from creative travel guide and today we share the things to know before you go to Mumbai looking for more travel tips make sure to hit subscribe and ring that notification Bell so let's look at the best time to visit winter is the best time to visit Mumbai with October to February offering moderate weather that is comfortable to walk around and explore the sights in March to May offers a more humid atmosphere to avoid this time if possible June to October is monsoon season with July and August having the most rainfall although it can be hard to see the sights at this time many say this is a good time for trekking and trips to see the nature outside of the city so now let's look at getting there many travel to Mumbai via air with the International Airport located around an hour outside of the city center direct flights to Mumbai are available for many cities in the world including Singapore Bangkok Hong Kong Dubai London and Paris from the airport you can take a taxi or train into the city center the Mumbai Central bus station is located in the center of the city and connects you to various destinations in the country whilst the train network is another great option for those traveling the country too so now let's look at getting around getting around Mumbai can be a challenge at times so planning ahead is essential the local train system is the best option for those wanting to travel around the train Network includes over 120 stops and has four main lines connecting you to most areas of the city center as well as some suburbs the bus system is also a great option as it connects you to many areas of the city too but traffic can cause delays especially during rush hour there are caps available in Mumbai which are fairly inexpensive and are easy to hail ideal for short distances Uber is available in Mumbai as well as a similar app called Ola they are great options for getting around the city and ideal for longer Journeys so now let's look at the typical prices dorm beds begin at 800 rupees whilst private rooms in hostels begin at 2000 mid-range hotel rooms and good locations begin at six thousand whilst luxury options start at around 12 000. many hotels and hostels include some type of simple breakfast but if not a street store breakfast can be found for under 200. lunch options range from 200 to 400 when eating in cheap local restaurants whilst fast food can be found from a similar price dinners in sit-down restaurants do vary depending on location and quality with local meals starting at 700 per person Mumbai is a great place for Budget Travelers because there are plenty of ways to save money firstly visit during the low season with the best prices being found from June to September Mumbai is a cash City so stick to using cash to avoid extra charges and ask your hostel or hotel staff for the best local restaurants nearby stick to traveling around using the train book accommodation in advance and make sure to eat from the local Street vendors and local restaurants for the best price Foods also avoid eating near their main attractions too so now let's look at the best things to do begin your trip with a visit to the stunning Taj Mahal Palace this is one of the most well-known buildings in the city and is a must-see during your visit next head to the Gateway of India for more impressive architecture followed by a trip to the gothic train station then take an evening stroll at the Marine Drive take a trip out to the elephanta caves a UNESCO world heritage site that can be explored in a day trip from Mumbai we will leave the link in the description below of a great tour option you could also spend a day exploring the Sanjay Gandhi National Park or spend time exploring one of the many museums visit the markets with Crawford Market being a fun popular option for visitors before tucking into some of the best local food you could choose to take a Mumbai street food tour too again the link is in the description take time to see the impressive mosques and temples in the city but also also see the Hanging Garden of Mumbai as well watch this video to find out the best places to visit in India then to find out 10 reasons to visit India watch this video we hope you have an incredible time and stay creative
